subject: Chemistry

learning_objectives.statement: Identify the rate law for a reaction from a mechanism in which the first step is not rate limiting.

depth_profile: Students must understand that if the first elementary reaction in a mechanism is not the rate-limiting step, approximations (such as the pre-equilibrium approximation) must be made to determine the overall rate law expression.
assessment_notes: This topic is assessed under the 'Mathematical Routines' skill (5.B), requiring students to identify an appropriate theory, definition, or mathematical relationship to solve a problem. On the AP Exam, students are expected to navigate between a proposed mechanism and a constructed rate law, applying the pre-equilibrium approximation to derive the rate law when the first step is fast and reversible.

spec_checklist.can_do: I can identify the rate law for a reaction from a mechanism in which the first step is not rate limiting.

spec_source_citation: AP Chemistry Course and Exam Description, V.1
